is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study concerns placental malaria caused by Plasmodium falciparum in Tanzania.

reports_target_markers.evidence: This is a human host-genetics study of the FLT1 gene dinucleotide repeat (rs3138582) and flanking SNPs; no P. falciparum drug-resistance markers (Pfcrt, Pfdhfr, Pfdhps, Pfk13, Pfmdr1, etc.) are studied.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Only human FLT1 allele/genotype frequencies are reported, not parasite drug-resistance codon frequencies.

sub_country_location.evidence: Muheza Designated District Hospital and Morogoro Regional Hospital, Tanzania — sub-national sites.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Deliveries September 2002 to April 2005; cord blood 2007.

african_field_samples.evidence: Field samples from Tanzanian mother-infant pairs.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Samples from the Mother-Offspring Malaria Studies Project cohort, used in multiple prior publications (refs 8, 14, 17).

supplement.evidence: No relevant drug-resistance marker data; supplementary tables concern FLT1 allele frequencies only.

assessment.summary: This is a human genetics study of the FLT1 gene and its selection under placental malaria in Tanzania. It does not report any P. falciparum drug-resistance markers or parasite genotype frequencies, so it is ineligible for this drug-resistance database.
exclusion_reasons: Does not report any curated P. falciparum drug-resistance markers
Reports human FLT1 host-genotype frequencies rather than parasite drug-resistance codon frequencies
